title: The Midnight Visitor – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Robert Arthur

questions.question: Who is Ausable?
questions.correct_answer: A secret agent living in Paris
questions.explanation: Ausable is an intelligence agent who appears ordinary but is highly intelligent and resourceful.

questions.question: Why is Fowler disappointed when he meets Ausable?
questions.correct_answer: Ausable does not look like a typical secret agent
questions.explanation: Fowler expected excitement and glamour, but Ausable appeared fat, slow, and ordinary.

questions.question: Who is Max?
questions.correct_answer: Another secret agent who threatens Ausable
questions.explanation: Max enters Ausable’s room with a gun to steal an important report.

questions.question: What important report is mentioned in the story?
questions.explanation: The report is valuable intelligence information that Max wants to steal.

questions.question: How does Ausable say Max entered the room?
questions.explanation: Ausable invents the story of a balcony to confuse and frighten Max.

questions.question: Why does Max believe the balcony story?
questions.correct_answer: Ausable describes it confidently and in detail
questions.explanation: The calm tone and detailed description make the lie convincing.

questions.question: Who actually knocks on the door at the end?
questions.explanation: There were no police; the knock was by a waiter bringing drinks.

questions.question: What happens to Max at the end of the story?
questions.correct_answer: He falls while trying to escape through the imaginary balcony
questions.explanation: Believing Ausable’s lie, Max steps out and meets his end.

questions.question: What quality of Ausable helps him defeat Max?
questions.explanation: Ausable remains calm and thinks quickly in a dangerous situation.

questions.question: What is the main message of the story?
questions.correct_answer: Intelligence and presence of mind are more powerful than force
questions.explanation: Ausable wins without violence by using intelligence and confidence.

notes.title: Presence of Mind
notes.content: The ability to stay calm and think clearly during danger is the central theme of the story.

notes.title: Deception as a Strategy
notes.content: Ausable uses lies and confidence instead of weapons to escape danger.

facts.fact: The balcony described by Ausable does not actually exist.
No physical fight occurs in the story.
